text: The flexible endoscopy showed an exophytic lesion located at the cervical portion of the trachea.
The patient is in the traditional position, sedated, spontaneously breathing and supported by an oxygen mask.
The trachea is exposed in the traditional fashion.
The limitation of the tracheal portion to be resected was achieved under direct vision using a flexible bronchoscope, marking the proximal and the distal margins of the segment to be resected.
If cough is detected during tracheal manipulation, sedation could be deepened under BIS monitoring.
Oxygen support is maintained in the distal tracheae.
Trachea is divided firstly at the distal limit of their section.
Oxygen support is set on the remaining distal trachea.
If the bleeding in the surgical field were not possible to be controlled, with some risk of aspiration in the distal portion of the tracheae, a tracheostomy tube could be placed to prevent it.
Margins are informed as free of disease by the pathology department.
A running suture with a 3-0 proline is used to perform the end-to-end tracheal anastomosis.
After completing the anastomosis, air leak is assessed by asking the anesthetist to provide positive pressure.
There was no evidence of air leakage.
A stitch is placed between the patient's chin and chest to prevent the extension of the neck.
